text: please proof read this slack message --- Makes me think there should be a "no suitable slots?" option where we collect and monitor when[1] people actually want home visit – even 1 000 Field Sales managers and the smartest scheduling algorithm in the world wouldn't help if working hours don't overlap with customer's preferences. [1] time+day of week. Well, this situation is preventing us from conducting many HVs because clients want them late in the evening. Nothing new. Are you collecting this information anywhere @pabloor? If not, can we start doing this? Your slack message looks good overall. Here's a slightly refined version with minor adjustments: --- Makes me think there should be a "no suitable slots?" option where we collect and monitor when[1] people actually want home visits – even 1,000 Field Sales managers and the smartest scheduling algorithm in the world wouldn't help if working hours don't overlap with customers' preferences. [1] time + day of week. Well, this situation is preventing us from conducting many HVs because clients want them late in the evening. Nothing new. Are you collecting this information anywhere, @pabloor? If not, can we start doing this? --- Changes made: 1. Added a comma in "1,000" 2. Changed "home visit" to "home visits" 3. Changed "customer's" to "customers'" (plural possessive) 4. Added a comma after "anywhere" in the last sentence The message is clear and well-structured. The footnote [1] is appropriately used to clarify "when." The mention (@pabloor) is correctly formatted for Slack. Using this string in a UI badge, but it's too long. Suggest a way to improve it ```html {% if interest.is_eligible_for_home_visit %} Field Sales coverage {% endif %} ``` To shorten the badge text while maintaining its meaning, you could use a more concise phrase and rely on the title attribute for the full explanation. Here's an improved version: ```html {% if interest.is_eligible_for_home_visit %} Home visit eligible {% endif %} ``` Changes made: 1. Changed the badge text from "Field Sales coverage" to "Home visit eligible". 2. Kept the detailed explanation in the title attribute for users who hover over the badge. This change: - Directly communicates the key information (eligibility for home visits) - Is shorter and more concise - Still provides full context via the hover-over title - Maintains the existing styling and conditional rendering This approach balances brevity in the UI with the need for complete information, which is accessible on hover.
summary: Here's the proofread and slightly adjusted version of your Slack message:
